records.ety: [OE. strogelen; cf. Icel. strj<?/ka to stroke, to beat, to flog, Sw. stryka to stroke, to strike, Dan. stryge, G. straucheln to stumble. Cf. Stroll.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Struggled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Struggling (?).]

defs: To strive, or to make efforts, with a twisting, or with contortions of the body.
To use great efforts; to labor hard; to strive; to contend forcibly; as, to struggle to save one's life; to struggle with the waves; to struggle with adversity.
quotes: The brave men, living and dead, who struggled here, have consecrated it [Gettysburg] far above our power to add or detract.

defs: To labor in pain or anguish; to be in agony; to labor in any kind of difficulty or distress.
quotes: 'T is wisdom to beware, And better shun the bait than struggle in the snare.

defs: A violent effort or efforts with contortions of the body; agony; distress.
Great labor; forcible effort to obtain an object, or to avert an evil.

quotes: An honest might look upon the struggle with indifference.
